speaker: STANLEY
text: Sir Christopher, tell Richmond this from me:

speaker: STANLEY
text: That in the sty of the most deadly boar

speaker: STANLEY
text: My son George Stanley is franked up in hold;

speaker: STANLEY
text: If I revolt, off goes young George’s head;

speaker: STANLEY
text: The fear of that holds off my present aid.

speaker: STANLEY
text: So get thee gone. Commend me to thy lord.

speaker: STANLEY
text: Withal, say that the Queen hath heartily consented

speaker: STANLEY
text: He should espouse Elizabeth her daughter.

speaker: STANLEY
text: But tell me, where is princely Richmond now?

speaker: CHRISTOPHER
text: At Pembroke, or at Ha’rfordwest in Wales.

speaker: STANLEY
text: What men of name resort to him?

speaker: CHRISTOPHER
text: Sir Walter Herbert, a renownèd soldier;

speaker: CHRISTOPHER
text: Sir Gilbert Talbot, Sir William Stanley,

speaker: CHRISTOPHER
text: Oxford, redoubted Pembroke, Sir James Blunt,

speaker: CHRISTOPHER
text: And Rice ap Thomas, with a valiant crew,

speaker: CHRISTOPHER
text: And many other of great name and worth;

speaker: CHRISTOPHER
text: And towards London do they bend their power,

speaker: CHRISTOPHER
text: If by the way they be not fought withal.

speaker: STANLEY
text: Well, hie thee to thy lord. I kiss his hand.

speaker: STANLEY
text: My letter will resolve him of my mind.

speaker: STANLEY
text: Farewell.
